concepts.name: Unit
concepts.description: The number one; the fundamental building block of all numbers.

concepts.name: Number
concepts.description: A multitude composed of units.

concepts.name: Counting
concepts.description: The process of determining the number of elements in a set.

concepts.name: Natural Numbers
concepts.description: The counting numbers: 1, 2, 3, 4, ...

concepts.name: Integers
concepts.description: Whole numbers including negatives: ..., -2, -1, 0, 1, 2, ...

concepts.name: Place Value
concepts.description: The value of a digit based on its position in a number.

concepts.name: Number Systems (Bases)
concepts.description: Different bases for representing numbers (binary, decimal, etc.).

concepts.name: Divisibility
concepts.description: When one number divides another without remainder.

concepts.name: Prime Numbers
concepts.description: Numbers greater than 1 with exactly two divisors: 1 and itself.

concepts.name: Point
concepts.description: That which has no part; a location with no size.

concepts.name: Line
concepts.description: Breadthless length; extends infinitely in both directions.

concepts.name: Angle
concepts.description: The inclination of two lines meeting at a point.

concepts.name: Right Angle
concepts.description: An angle of 90 degrees; when a line stands on another making equal adjacent angles.

concepts.name: Triangle
concepts.description: A polygon with three sides and three angles.

concepts.name: Right Triangle
concepts.description: A triangle with one right angle.

concepts.name: Area
concepts.description: The measure of the size of a two-dimensional surface.

concepts.name: Square
concepts.description: A rectangle with four equal sides and four right angles.

concepts.name: Pythagorean Theorem
concepts.description: In a right triangle, a² + b² = c² where c is the hypotenuse.

concepts.name: Function
concepts.description: A relation that assigns exactly one output to each input.

concepts.name: Polynomial
concepts.description: An expression of variables and coefficients with addition, subtraction, and multiplication.

concepts.name: Quadratic Function
concepts.description: A polynomial of degree 2: f(x) = ax² + bx + c.

concepts.name: Parabola
concepts.description: The graph of a quadratic function; a U-shaped curve.

concepts.name: Number Line
concepts.description: A visual representation of numbers on a straight line.

concepts.name: Harmonic Series in Music
concepts.description: The mathematical relationship between overtones: integer multiples of a fundamental frequency that determine timbre and consonance.

concepts.name: Pythagorean Tuning
concepts.description: A tuning system based on stacking pure fifths (3:2 ratio). Reveals the Pythagorean comma — the impossibility of closing the circle of fifths with pure intervals.

concepts.name: Fibonacci & Golden Ratio in Music
concepts.description: The Fibonacci sequence appears in the chromatic scale (13 notes, 8 white, 5 black, 3+2 grouping) and the golden ratio governs climax placement in compositions.

concepts.name: Mozart's Musical Dice Game
concepts.description: A combinatorial composition system (K. 516f) where two dice select from 176 pre-composed measures to generate 11^16 unique minuets.

concepts.name: Euclidean Rhythms
concepts.description: Distributing k beats among n steps using Euclid's algorithm produces rhythms found across world music traditions.

concepts.name: Group Theory in Music
concepts.description: Musical transformations (transposition, inversion, retrograde) form algebraic groups. The 48 forms of a tone row in serial music are generated by group operations.

concepts.name: Fractal Music & Self-Similarity
concepts.description: Self-similar structures in music, from Bach's canons to algorithmic composition using L-systems and 1/f noise.

concepts.name: Wave Equation & Acoustics
concepts.description: The partial differential equation governing sound propagation, standing waves, and resonant frequencies of musical instruments.

concepts.name: Fast Fourier Transform
concepts.description: The Cooley-Tukey algorithm computes the DFT in O(N log N) time via divide-and-conquer on even/odd indices, exploiting roots of unity symmetry.

concepts.name: Inverse FFT & Signal Reconstruction
concepts.description: The inverse DFT recovers a time-domain signal from its frequency representation, using the unitarity of the DFT matrix.

concepts.name: Frequency Domain Filtering
concepts.description: Filtering via the convolution theorem: multiply spectra in the frequency domain instead of convolving in time, reducing O(N²) to O(N log N).

concepts.name: 2D FFT & Image Processing
concepts.description: The separable 2D DFT decomposes images into spatial frequencies. Low frequencies encode gradients, high frequencies encode edges and textures.

concepts.name: Fourier Transform & Image Compression
concepts.description: DCT-based compression (JPEG) and the Fourier Slice Theorem (CT/MRI reconstruction) connect frequency analysis to practical image processing.
